original_reasoning: Golden commonly carries the sense of beauty, excellence, and attractive quality, close to حسن at the surface level.
الخسيج means a tent or pavilion, and λέχεσσι means beds/lairs; both converge on an enclosed resting place or lodging.
Arabic core is letting go, sending forth, or allowing passage; Greek likely belongs to release/loosening, which is a strong conceptual match.
Inability and weakness in عجز align well with slackening, relaxing, and becoming lazy.
Arabic شجر between people means dispute or clash, and Greek means collide; this is a clear direct match on the conflict side of the concept.
Arabic التام الحسن and Greek beauty are a direct semantic match at the surface level.
حري includes aiming toward or intending a side; that deepens cleanly into setting oneself to an act, hence 'attempt'.
Arabic qaws is bow or curve; Greek helix is something twisted or spiral. The curved form is clearly preserved.
